findings.title: Jenkins CI/CD accessible without authentication
findings.description: Jenkins dashboard is accessible on port 8080 without requiring authentication. An unauthenticated user can view build history, environment variables, and potentially execute arbitrary build steps.
findings.recommendation: Enable Jenkins authentication immediately. Configure Matrix-based security. Restrict network access to Jenkins to VPN or internal network only.

findings.title: Intranet served over HTTP (unencrypted)
findings.description: The corporate intranet is served over plain HTTP on port 80. Any credentials or session tokens transmitted are exposed to network-level interception.
findings.recommendation: Migrate the intranet to HTTPS. Obtain a certificate from the internal CA and enforce TLS. Add HSTS headers after migration.

findings.title: Cisco AnyConnect VPN version disclosed
findings.description: The VPN portal discloses the Cisco AnyConnect version in its HTML source. This assists an attacker in identifying known CVEs for the deployed version.
findings.recommendation: Configure the VPN portal to suppress version disclosure in HTTP responses and HTML source.

findings.title: Development host exposed in certificate transparency logs
findings.description: A certificate was issued for dev-internal.acme.com and recorded in public certificate transparency logs. This reveals an internal development host to external observers.
findings.recommendation: Use an internal CA for development certificates to avoid exposing internal hostnames in public CT logs.
